title: Wet-felted journal cover
subtitle: A merino cover for an A5 notebook, with a folded flap that slips over the front and back boards
excerpt: Wet-felt a flat rectangle of merino roving large enough to wrap around an A5 notebook and fold over the boards. The cover slips on and off without glue or stitching and stretches to grip the boards once the felt has dried on the book.
sourceNotes: Book-cover felting is a standard project in European craft workshops. The fold-over pocket method for gripping boards without adhesive is adapted from traditional fabric book-cloth binding techniques. Observation-led.

glossaryTerms.definition: Combed and drawn wool fibres in a continuous rope, ready for felting. Wisps pulled from the roving are laid in crossing layers before wetting and agitation to form felt.
The aggressive working stage of wet felting in which already-locked fibres are compressed further to achieve the final density and dimensions.

content.text: A wet-felted journal cover wraps an A5 notebook in a layer of dense merino

felt with front and back pockets that slip over the boards. The finished cover moves with the book as it flexes: dense merino felt has enough drape to open flat without cracking. The cover is made as a flat rectangle with wide wings at each end that are folded back to form the pockets. Sized for a standard A5 notebook (14.8 x 21 cm); the instructions include how to adjust for other sizes.

items.name: Merino roving, 21 to 24 micron, 120 g (allows for shrinkage and offcuts)
Contrasting roving for decoration, optional, 20 g
Hot water and olive-oil soap or wool wash
Bamboo rolling mat, at least 50 x 60 cm
A5 notebook (for sizing and blocking), 14.8 x 21 cm

content.text: Measure the notebook: height (H), width (W), and spine thickness (S). The felt rectangle you lay must be, before felting: height = H x 1.6, width = (W x 2 + S + 10 cm for pockets) x 1.6. For a standard A5 notebook (21 x 14.8 x 1 cm spine): height layout = 33 cm, width layout = 55 cm. After felting and
to 40 percent shrinkage, the finished piece will measure approximately 21 x 34 cm, allowing 5 cm pockets at each end and the spine plus cover area in the middle.

Mark the pre-felt rectangle dimensions on the rolling mat. Lay roving in two crossing layers across the full rectangle, building to an even 1 cm thickness throughout. Add decorative wisps in a contrast colour on the top surface if desired, laying them in stripes or patches. Do not decorate the pocket wings (the 8 to 9 cm sections at each end) as these will fold inward and be hidden.
Step 2: Wet, press, and begin felting

Wet thoroughly with hot soapy water, cover with the tea towel, and press flat with the palms. Rub for 10 minutes through the cloth, then rub directly on the wet surface for another 10 minutes. The felt should begin to hold together as a sheet before you start rolling.
Step 3: Roll and full to size

Roll the sheet in the mat and roll the bundle back and forth 50 times. Unroll, turn 90 degrees, roll 50 more. Repeat, alternating directions, until the sheet has shrunk to approximately 40 percent in each dimension. Check by measuring against the notebook: the felt should wrap the book with 5 cm extension at each end for the pockets. If the felt is still too large, continue rolling. Shock with cold water every three or four rolling sequences to accelerate shrinkage.
Step 4: Shape and block on the notebook

While damp, lay the felt flat and trim the edges straight. Fold 5 cm at each end back against the outside of the felt (wrong side to wrong side) to form the two pockets. Slip the notebook inside: slide the front board into the front pocket and the back board into the back pocket. Smooth the cover over the book so the felt wraps evenly around the spine. Leave to dry on the book for 24 hours. Once dry, the cover holds the book's shape and the pockets grip the boards.

items.problem: The cover is too small and does not wrap the notebook
items.cause: The felt shrank more than 40 percent during fulling
items.fix: Wet the cover in warm water and stretch it gently in all directions while damp, then re-block on the book. Dense merino felt has limited stretch; significant undersizing cannot be fully corrected and the piece may need to cover a smaller notebook.

items.problem: The surface feels thin and flexible rather than firm
items.cause: Insufficient fulling; the felt did not reach full density
items.fix: Rewet the cover in hot soapy water and continue rolling aggressively for another 10 to 15 minutes. A journal cover should feel stiff enough to stand upright briefly when placed on its edge.

items.problem: The decorative surface details have shifted or blurred
items.cause: The decoration wisps were placed on top rather than integrated into the top layer
items.fix: For future pieces, lay the decoration wisps first, then add a thin top layer of the main colour over them. The main layer locks the decoration in place during rolling.
